q: Audibility refers to the ability to hear or detect sounds within any range of frequencies.
The human ear can detect sounds across an unlimited range of frequencies.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit of audibility is 20 Hz.
Sounds with frequencies above 20,000 Hz are known as infrasound.
Infrasound can be heard by humans but only at very low volumes.
Ultrasound refers to sounds that are below the lower limit of human audibility.
A vibrating ruler that is seen but not heard produces ultrasound.
Bats and whales primarily use infrasound for echolocation.
Most sonar technologies rely on the use of infrasound.
If a dog reacts to a whistle that a human cannot hear, it means the whistle produces infrasound.
Ultrasound is used in quality control to check for cracks in concrete.
Prenatal ultrasound tests use low-frequency sound waves that are easily audible to the human ear.
In a prenatal ultrasound test, echoes are recorded and transformed into video or photographic images.
Ultrasonic scalers in dentistry remove plaque by chemically dissolving it.
Ultrasonic cleaning is most effective for smooth, regular surfaces.
Cavitation bubbles, created by high energy ultrasound, help displace contaminants during ultrasonic cleaning.
The upper limit of audibility for humans is typically around 20 Hz.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is called the 'limit of audibility'.
Sounds with frequencies below 20 Hz are termed 'audible frequencies' for humans.
Ultrasound is used to inspect metal pipes and measure the thickness of wooden boards.
In prenatal ultrasound, a transducer transmits sound waves through the abdomen.
Ultrasonic scalers in dentistry use high-frequency ultrasound to mechanically remove calculus deposits.
Ultrasonic cleaning involves creating cavitation bubbles at sites of compression in the sound wave.
Humans can hear sounds with frequencies of 25,000 Hz.
The audible frequency range can be determined by gradually tuning sound frequencies from 0 Hz upwards using a frequency generator.
Infrasound is characterized by short wavelengths and high frequencies.
Sonar technologies often utilize infrasound to detect objects.
Ultrasonic cleaning is particularly effective for cleaning internal cavities.
The lower limit of human audibility is 20,000 Hz.
The sound produced by a dog whistle is usually within the human audible range.
The primary function of a prenatal ultrasound test is to provide therapeutic treatment for the fetus.
The term 'infrasound' literally means 'above sound'.
Ultrasound waves used in medical imaging are typically audible to the human ear.
Quality control applications of ultrasound are limited to checking for cracks in concrete.
Cavitation bubbles are created by low energy ultrasound.
The upper limit of audibility for humans is 20 Hz.
A frequency generator is a tool that can be used to determine a person's audible frequency range.
When cleaning with ultrasound, cavitation bubbles help displace contaminants.
Ultrasound is used in dentistry to chemically soften plaque on teeth.
The sound waves used in a prenatal ultrasound test are transmitted via a device called a transducer.
Infrasound waves are known for their ability to travel over short distances only.
Ultrasound technology is frequently employed in quality control for inspecting the internal structure of materials.
The frequency range of human audibility extends from 0 Hz to 20,000 Hz.
The term 'ultrasound' refers to sound waves with frequencies greater than 20,000 Hz.
Infrasound is used by bats for navigation and locating prey.
Medical ultrasonic cleaners utilize high energy ultrasound for effective cleaning.
The sound from a vibrating ruler, if inaudible but visible, is an example of ultrasound.
